privilege.name: AI Query Patient Data
privilege.description: Allows querying patient charts using the AI-powered chart search

privilege.name: View AI Audit Logs
privilege.description: Allows viewing the audit log of AI chart search queries

globalProperty.description: LLM inference engine. "local" (default) runs a GGUF model in-process via llama.cpp — no network calls, data stays on the server. "remote" calls an OpenAI-compatible chat completions API — requires chartsearchai.llm.remote.endpointUrl, chartsearchai.llm.remote.apiKey, and chartsearchai.llm.remote.modelName to be configured. Use "remote" for self-hosted GPU inference servers (vLLM, Ollama, text-generation-inference) or cloud-hosted models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI).

URL of the OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint. Required when chartsearchai.llm.engine is "remote". Examples: http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions (Ollama), http://gpu-server:8000/v1/chat/completions (vLLM), https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions (OpenAI), https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/chat/completions (Google AI).

Model name to request from the remote LLM endpoint. Required when chartsearchai.llm.engine is "remote". Examples: llama3.3 (Ollama), meta-llama/Llama-3.3-8B-Instruct (vLLM), gpt-4o (OpenAI), gemini-2.5-pro (Google AI).

Relative path, including the filename, within the OpenMRS application data directory to the GGUF LLM model file used for inference. Example: chartsearchai/medgemma-1.5-4b-it-Q4_K_M.gguf (resolves to <application-data-directory>/chartsearchai/medgemma-1.5-4b-it-Q4_K_M.gguf). The recommended model is MedGemma 1.5 4B Instruct (Q4_K_M quantization, ~2.5GB). Download from https://huggingface.co/unsloth/medgemma-1.5-4b-it-GGUF. Path traversal (e.g. "..") is not allowed.

When true, uses embedding similarity to pre-filter patient records to the most relevant ones before sending to the LLM. Embeddings are indexed on first patient access and kept up to date automatically. Set to false to send the full patient chart to the LLM without any embedding-based filtering. Default: true.

Selects the retrieval pipeline used to pre-filter patient records before sending them to the LLM. Options: "embedding" (default) uses vector similarity via an ONNX model with custom scoring; "lucene" uses Apache Lucene BM25 text search; "hybrid" combines Lucene BM25 and embedding kNN search using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) — same quality as the Elasticsearch pipeline but with no external services required; "elasticsearch" uses Elasticsearch hybrid search combining BM25 text and kNN vector search via RRF (requires Elasticsearch 8.14+ configured in OpenMRS). All require chartsearchai.embedding.preFilter to be true. Records are indexed automatically on first access. Changing this setting takes effect on the next query.
Chat template for formatting prompts sent to the LLM. Use a preset name (llama3, mistral, phi3, chatml, gemma) or a custom template string with {system} and {user} placeholders. Default: gemma.
System prompt sent to the LLM before patient records and the question. Customize this to adjust the LLM's behavior, tone, or citation format.
Maximum number of seconds to wait for the LLM to generate a response before timing out.

Minutes of inactivity after which the local LLM model is unloaded from memory to free RAM. The model is automatically reloaded on the next query. Set to 0 to keep the model loaded indefinitely. Only applies when chartsearchai.llm.engine is "local".
Maximum number of AI chart search queries a single user can make per minute. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.
Number of days to retain AI chart search audit log entries. Entries older than this are automatically purged by a daily scheduled task. Set to 0 to disable purging and retain all logs indefinitely.
Number of minutes to cache AI chart search responses for identical patient and question combinations. Avoids redundant LLM calls. Set to 0 to disable caching (default).

Maximum number of records to retrieve via embedding similarity when pre-filtering is enabled. Lower values produce more focused LLM responses; higher values provide more context but may reduce reasoning quality. Default: 10.

Minimum similarity score as a fraction of the top result's score. Records scoring below this ratio are excluded even if within the topK limit. For example, 0.80 means a record must have at least 80% of the best match's similarity score. Lower values include more records; higher values are more selective. Must be between 0 and 1. Default: 0.80.

Controls adaptive topK by detecting natural cluster boundaries in similarity scores. When the gap between consecutive scores exceeds this multiplier times the running average gap, the cluster boundary is found and lower-scoring records are excluded. Higher values (e.g. 3.0) are more permissive (include more records); lower values (e.g. 2.0) are more aggressive at cutting. Set to a very large value (e.g. 999) to effectively disable gap detection and rely solely on the similarity ratio threshold. Default: 2.5.

Additive keyword bonus weight in the hybrid retrieval formula: finalScore = semanticScore + weight * keywordScore. Keyword overlap can only increase the score, never decrease it. Set to 0 to disable keyword matching. Default: 0.3.

Score multiplier applied to records whose resource type matches the query intent (e.g., drug orders when the query is about medications). Set to 1.0 to disable type boosting (default). Values like 1.2-1.5 provide moderate boosting. Must be between 1.0 and 3.0. Default: 1.0.

Prefix prepended to the user query before embedding. Leave empty for models like all-MiniLM-L6-v2 that were not trained with instruction prefixes. Set to "search_query: " or "Represent this sentence for searching relevant passages: " for models that support instruction-aware queries (e.g., BGE). Default: empty.
Maximum WordPiece token sequence length for embedding input. Increase when using models that support longer contexts (e.g., 512 for BGE models). Must be between 32 and 8192. Default: 256.

Minimum absolute gap between consecutive similarity scores required for the adaptive cutoff detector to trigger. Prevents premature cutting when a relatively large gap (compared to a tight cluster's running average) is still small in absolute terms. Only applies when gap detection is active via chartsearchai.embedding.scoreGapMultiplier. Default: 0.10.

Cosine similarity threshold for validating whether a gap in the score distribution is intra-topic (e.g. different vital sign types in the same broad category) or inter-topic (a real relevance boundary). When the average cosine between records just above and below the gap meets or exceeds this threshold, the gap is considered intra-topic and the second-pass cut is skipped. Lower values are more permissive (more gaps treated as intra-topic); higher values are more aggressive at cutting. Must be between 0 and 1. Default: 0.47.

Relative path, including the filename, within the OpenMRS application data directory to the ONNX cross-encoder reranker model file. When configured, a cross-encoder reranking stage is added after embedding retrieval to improve result quality by scoring (query, document) pairs jointly. Leave empty to disable reranking (default). Example: chartsearchai/cross-encoder-model.onnx. Path traversal (e.g. "..") is not allowed.

Relative path, including the filename, within the OpenMRS application data directory to the WordPiece vocabulary file for the cross-encoder reranker model. Required when chartsearchai.reranker.modelFilePath is configured. Example: chartsearchai/cross-encoder-vocab.txt. Path traversal (e.g. "..") is not allowed.

Maximum WordPiece token sequence length for cross-encoder reranker input. The query and document are concatenated as a sentence pair; the document is truncated if the total exceeds this limit. Must be between 32 and 8192. Default: 512.

Relative path, including the filename, within the OpenMRS application data directory to the ONNX embedding model file. Required when embedding pre-filtering is enabled (chartsearchai.embedding.preFilter is true, the default). Example: chartsearchai/all-MiniLM-L6-v2.onnx (resolves to <application-data-directory>/chartsearchai/all-MiniLM-L6-v2.onnx). Download the model.onnx file from https://huggingface.co/sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 and rename it. Path traversal (e.g. "..") is not allowed.

Relative path, including the filename, within the OpenMRS application data directory to the WordPiece vocabulary file (vocab.txt) for the ONNX embedding model. Required when embedding pre-filtering is enabled (chartsearchai.embedding.preFilter is true, the default). Example: chartsearchai/vocab.txt (resolves to <application-data-directory>/chartsearchai/vocab.txt). Download vocab.txt from the same model repository as the ONNX model (e.g. https://huggingface.co/sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2). Path traversal (e.g. "..") is not allowed.
